title: 10 best herbal capsules for burning feet
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for burning feet are a natural remedy designed to provide relief from burning sensations, pain, and discomfort in the feet. They typically contain a blend of herbs such as cayenne pepper, capsicum, and willow bark that help reduce inflammation and ease nerve pain. Capsules like Neuriva and Pain-X contain these herbal extracts, which have been shown to improve lives by providing instant relief from burning feet, allowing individuals to engage in daily activities without discomfort. This can be especially beneficial for people with conditions like diabetic neuropathy or athlete's foot, who often experience persistent burning sensations in their feet.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper capsules helps with burning feet because they contain a unique blend of bioactive compounds, including capsaicin, vitamin C, and antioxidants. These nutrients work together to reduce inflammation and ease pain in the feet, providing relief from the discomfort and tingling sensation associated with burning feet. The anti-inflammatory properties also help to improve circulation, allowing oxygen and nutrients to reach the affected areas more efficiently.
remedy_properties: Ginger: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce pain and swelling in the feet.
Capsaicin: Found in chili peppers, it has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve pain and discomfort in the feet.
Peppermint: Has cooling and soothing effects when applied topically, which can provide temporary relief from burning sensations in the feet.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin helps with burning feet by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which is responsible for transmitting pain signals to the brain, thereby reducing the sensation of burning or pain.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, help with burning feet by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to the burning sensation and discomfort.

Phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid): Phenolic acids help with burning feet by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the burning sensation and promote healing of the affected areas.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits of Capsicum annuum are used to make capsules for burning feet, as they contain the highest concentration of capsaicinoids, which produce the burning sensation.
Seeds: Capsicum annuum seeds are used for this purpose, as they can be harvested from the fruits and contain a higher concentration of capsaicinoids than the fruits themselves.
Leaves: The leaves of Capsicum annuum may be used, as they contain some capsaicinoids, although in lower concentrations than the fruits or seeds.
remedy_recipe: Dry the ripe capsicum annuum fruits in a single layer at 50°c for 3 days.
Grind 500g of dried capsicum annuum into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 300g of powdered capsicum annuum with 100g of vegetable glycerin.
Fill 2000 empty gelatin capsules with 300mg of the capsicum annuum mixture each.
Store the capsicum annuum capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with burning feet because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules contain compounds that penetrate deep into the skin, targeting the root cause of discomfort and relieving pressure on sensitive nerve endings. As a result, they reduce swelling and inflammation, providing fast and effective relief from burning sensations in the feet. Additionally, ginger's warming properties also help to increase blood flow, which further contributes to its ability to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations commonly associated with burning feet.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and pain in the feet, alleviating burning sensations.
Pain-relieving properties: The analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects of ginger help to numb the burning sensation, providing relief from discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress, which can exacerbate burning feet symptoms by damaging tissue and nerves.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate burning sensations in the feet by reducing pain and inflammation.
Shogaols: Shogaols, especially 6-shogaol, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help soothe burning feet by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area.

Volatile oils (e.g., Zingiberene and Bisabolol): The volatile oils present in ginger capsules, such as zingiberene and bisabolol, exhibit anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antioxidant properties that help to calm burning sensations in the feet by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain essential oils that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving burning feet.
Buds: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which contribute to their pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties, useful in treating burning feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of fresh zingiber officinale roots and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 10 grams of vegetable cellulose or gelatin to create a uniform mixture.
Fill 100 empty capsules with 0.5 grams of the herbal mixture and seal them properly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with burning feet because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, reduces inflammation that can cause burning sensations on the soles of the feet. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate pain and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, turmeric capsules provide natural relief from burning feet, allowing individuals to enjoy improved comfort and mobility in their daily lives.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric capsules contain curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the feet.

Cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2) inhibition: Curcuminoids in turmeric capsules have been shown to inhibit COX-2, an enzyme that contributes to pain and inflammation, thereby helping to alleviate burning foot sensations.
Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage in the feet, which may exacerbate burning sensations.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, helps alleviate burning feet by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to burning sensations.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, a bioactive compound found in Curcuma longa, may help with burning feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules and reducing pain sensitivity.
Cadinene: Cadinene, a sesquiterpene, may contribute to the analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of Curcuma longa, which can help alleviate burning sensations in the feet.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the most commonly used part of Curcuma longa due to their high concentration of curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate burning sensations in the feet.
Roots: The roots are also used as an alternative to rhyzomes, as they contain similar compounds that can provide relief from burning sensations.
Stems: The stems are less commonly used than rhyzomes and roots, but they still contain some of the same compounds that can help alleviate burning sensations.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried curcuma longa roots, grind them into powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 part of the ground curcuma longa powder with 2 parts of gelatin in a bowl.
Add 100 ml of distilled water to the bowl and heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the curcuma longa mixture using a small spoon.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place to preserve their medicinal properties for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow capsules helps with burning feet because they contain salicin, a natural compound that reduces inflammation and relieves pain. When taken orally, the salicin in white willow capsules is absorbed into the bloodstream and reaches the skin, where it targets areas of inflammation and discomfort, such as burning feet caused by conditions like peripheral neuropathy or arthritis. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of salicin provide effective relief from burning sensations, allowing individuals to find comfort and normalcy in their daily lives.
remedy_properties: Salicin: White willow capsules contain salicin, a natural pain reliever that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate burning sensations in the feet by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal extract in white willow capsules has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and redness associated with burning feet, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Analgesic properties: White willow capsules have analgesic properties that help to numb the affected area, reducing the sensation of burning or tingling in the feet, making them a natural alternative to over-the-counter pain relievers.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: Salicin is a salicylic acid derivative that acts as a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce the pain and discomfort associated with burning feet.

Tannins: Tannins in Salix alba have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and alleviate the burning sensation in the feet by constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid present in Salix alba, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the feet, leading to relief from burning sensations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Salix alba are often used to make a salve or tincture that may help to soothe and cool the skin.
Buds: The buds of Salix alba are sometimes used to make a salve or tincture that may help to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the salix alba bark by selecting branches from mature trees with a diameter of at least 10 cm.
Dry the harvested bark for 2-3 days in a cool, well-ventilated area to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes to ensure uniform consistency.
Mix the powder with gelatin and distilled water according to a ratio of 1:2:0.5 to form a capsule filling.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5 grams of the prepared mixture and seal the capsules immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with burning feet because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This natural ingredient helps to reduce inflammation in the feet, which is often the underlying cause of burning sensations. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to have a soothing effect on irritated skin and nerves, providing quick relief from discomfort and pain associated with burning feet. By addressing the root cause of the issue, herbal licorice capsules offer a natural and effective solution for alleviating burning foot symptoms.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate burning sensations by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can cause pain and inflammation.

Flavonoids: Act as powerful antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to burning foot symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and pain.

Saponins: Possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce swelling and relieve pain associated with burning feet, making it a natural remedy for conditions like peripheral neuropathy.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules help with burning feet by reducing inflammation and soothing irritated nerve endings, which can contribute to burning sensations.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate burning feet by reducing swelling and pain associated with inflammation.

Liquiritin: Liquiritin, a flavonoid glycoside, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect nerve cells from damage and reduce burning sensations caused by oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory properties to alleviate burning sensations in the feet.
Roots: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in the roots, helps reduce inflammation and soothe burning feet.
Roots: The roots are a rich source of glycyrrhizin, which is used to create capsules that help alleviate burning sensations and provide relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the early morning and clean them thoroughly with water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm and well-ventilated area at 40°c for 48 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder at 5000 rpm for 30 seconds.
Mix 500 mg of the root powder with 200 mg of gelatin and 20 mg of magnesium stearate in a small bowl.
Fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules using an automatic capsule filling machine at 10 capsules per minute.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw capsules helps with burning feet because they contain harpagoside, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and calms irritated skin. This herb has been traditionally used to relieve pain and discomfort associated with arthritic conditions, making it an effective remedy for burning foot syndrome. By reducing inflammation and alleviating pressure on the nerves, devil's claw capsules provide instant relief from burning sensations, numbness, and tingling in the feet, allowing individuals to regain comfort and mobility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Devil's Claw contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the feet and relieve burning sensations.
Pain-relieving: The herb has been shown to have analgesic and anesthetic effects, which can help numb or reduce pain associated with burning feet.
Antioxidant: Devil's Claw contains antioxidants that can help protect the feet from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by free radicals, further reducing discomfort and pain.
remedy_constituents: Harpagoside: A glycoside compound, harpagoside has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help reduce burning sensations in the feet by addressing underlying inflammation.

Alkaloids (specifically, harpagide and harpagogenin): These compounds have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate burning pain and discomfort in the feet.

Triterpenoids (specifically, harpagoic acid): Triterpenoids have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the feet from oxidative stress and inflammation, contributing to burning sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make capsules for burning feet because they contain high concentrations of harpagoside, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks are used to make capsules for burning feet because they are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help soothe and calm burning sensations.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make capsules for burning feet because they contain harpagoside and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest harpagophytum procumbens roots in late autumn to early winter when the plant is fully mature.
Clean and dry the harvested roots by spreading them out in a warm dry place for 7 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 400-800 mg of the ground root powder using a powder scoop for 2 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with burning feet because of its potent antinociceptive properties. The herb contains active compounds such as hyperforin, which has been shown to reduce pain perception and inflammation in the body. When taken orally, St John's Wort can help alleviate the burning sensation in the feet by targeting the underlying causes of neuropathic pain, such as nerve damage or irritation. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to reducing swelling and discomfort in the affected area.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This bioactive compound has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the feet.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort, such as hyperoside and quercetin, have antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing burning sensations.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids like beta-amyrin have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of burning feet such as itching and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin is a terpenoid compound that helps with burning feet by reducing inflammation and pain due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.

Hypericin: Hypericin is a naphthodianthrone that helps with burning feet by blocking the reuptake of neurotransmitters like serotonin and norepinephrine, which can contribute to pain perception and may help alleviate burning sensations.
Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid phenolic compound that helps with burning feet by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to burning sensations and pain in the feet.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make capsules for burning feet due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Flowers: Used to make capsules for burning feet due to their analgesic and antiseptic properties, which help reduce pain and prevent infection.
Seeds: Used to make capsules for burning feet due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce pain and prevent infection, as well as promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the hypericum perforatum flowers at their peak potency during the summer months when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to release their active compounds.
Mix the powdered flowers with a natural capsule filler such as gelatin or cellulose in a 1:1 ratio by weight.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared mixture and seal them immediately to preserve the potency of the hypericum perforatum.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with burning feet because they contain a natural compound called valerenic acid, which has a calming effect on the nervous system. This can help to reduce nerve sensitivity and ease discomfort in the feet, often caused by conditions such as peripheral neuropathy or diabetes. The herbal supplement also promotes relaxation and reduces muscle tension, allowing for improved blood flow and circulation to the affected areas. As a result, valerian capsules provide effective relief from burning sensations in the feet, promoting overall comfort and well-being.
remedy_properties: Sedative and relaxant properties: Valerian root helps to calm the nervous system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with burning feet.
Antispasmodic properties: Valerian's antispasmodic effects can help relieve muscle cramps and spasms in the feet, which may be contributing to the burning sensation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian has anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce swelling and inflammation in the feet, helping to alleviate burning sensations.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: It has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate burning sensations and reduce inflammation in the feet.
Valeranone: This terpene has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which may help to calm down the nervous activity that can cause burning sensations in the feet.
Isovaleric acid: It has a local anesthetic effect, which can numb the pain and discomfort associated with burning feet, providing relief from the sensation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain valerenic acid and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to soothe burning feet.

Leaves: They have been traditionally used to make teas that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which may contribute to burning feet, and their anti-inflammatory properties can also help alleviate pain.
Rhyzomes: They contain compounds similar to those found in the roots, such as valerenic acid, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate burning sensations in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in the fall after the first frost when the plant is dormant.
Clean and dry the roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 2-3 weeks.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250-500 mg of the powder with a binder like gelatin or cellulose in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a small scoop or spoon.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with burning feet because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The capsules soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with burning sensations. Additionally, the antioxidant-rich extracts help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to foot pain, promoting overall foot health and relief from burning feet. Regular use of maidenhair tree capsules can provide long-lasting comfort and relief for those affected by burning feet.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to the relief of burning foot sensations.
Vasodilatory effects: The extract's ability to dilate blood vessels can improve circulation, which may help increase oxygen delivery to the feet and reduce feelings of burning or numbness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory compounds, which could potentially reduce inflammation and discomfort in the feet.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: A triterpene lactone that helps reduce inflammation and improves blood flow to the feet, which may alleviate burning sensations caused by poor circulation.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin and Kaempferol): A type of phenolic compound that have potent antioxidant properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the feet, thereby alleviating burning sensations.

Ginkgolides: A class of diterpene trilactones that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce pain and discomfort in the feet by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to improve blood flow to the feet and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are used to make capsules for burning feet as they have anti-inflammatory properties and can help to reduce pain and discomfort.
Barks: Ginkgo barks are sometimes used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1000g of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees when they are 4-6 years old.
Dry the leaves in a clean and well-ventilated area at 50-60c for 2-3 hours.
Grind 500g of dried leaves into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 2g of the ginkgo biloba powder with 8g of vegetable cellulose to form a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty capsules with 150mg of the ginkgo biloba-vegetable cellulose blend each and seal them properly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew capsules helps with burning feet because they contain parthenolide, a natural compound that reduces inflammation and eases discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of feverfew capsules work by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and swelling in the body. By reducing these prostaglandins, feverfew capsules can help alleviate the burning sensation, numbness, and tingling often associated with burning feet. Additionally, feverfew's cooling effect can also help soothe and calm the skin, providing long-lasting relief from this common discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew contains parthenolide, which has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the feet.
Analgesic and anesthetic properties: The volatile oils present in feverfew have analgesic and anesthetic properties, which can help numb pain and relieve burning sensations in the feet.
Antioxidant activity: Feverfew's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to burning foot symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Feverfew roots contain parthenolide, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and soothe burning sensations in the feet.
Barks: The barks of the feverfew plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate burning foot symptoms by reducing swelling and pain.
remedy_constituents: Parthenolide: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and discomfort associated with burning feet.
Bornediol: This sesquiterpene lactone has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort in the feet.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the feet, providing relief from burning sensations.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 10 grams of tanacetum parthenium dried flowers and leaves.
Grind the measured tanacetum parthenium flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a grinder.
Mix the powder with 10 grams of gelatin and 5 milliliters of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the gelatin mixture and seal them tightly.
Label and store the herbal tanacetum parthenium capsules in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with burning feet because of its natural cooling properties that can soothe and calm irritated skin. The menthol in peppermint oil has a numbing effect, which can temporarily reduce the sensation of burning or tingling. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with burning feet. By taking herbal peppermint capsules, individuals can experience relief from burning foot symptoms, promoting overall comfort and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and methyl salicylate, help to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with burning feet, providing relief from discomfort.

Pain-relieving properties: The active ingredients in peppermint, including menthone and limonene, have analgesic and anesthetic effects, helping to numb the affected area and alleviate the sensation of burning.

Cooling and soothing properties: Peppermint's cooling and soothing properties help to reduce heat, itching, and tingling sensations associated with burning feet, providing immediate relief and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other bioactive compounds, peppermint roots are effective in relieving itching, inflammation, and discomfort associated with burning feet.
Rhizomes: The underground stem of peppermint plants contains a high concentration of calming agents that help reduce pain and numbness in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality peppermint leaves from a trusted source and dry them thoroughly for at least three days.
Grind 20g of dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Combine the powdered peppermint with 50g of gelatin and mix well in a small bowl until uniform.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the peppermint-gelatin mixture and seal them immediately to prevent moisture absorption.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for up to six months or freeze for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense capsules helps with burning feet because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and pain in the feet. The capsules also promote healthy circulation, allowing for a more efficient delivery of oxygen and nutrients to the affected areas. Additionally, frankincense has natural antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent infection and promote healing. By taking these capsules regularly, individuals with burning feet may experience relief from discomfort and improved overall foot health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the skin, which can alleviate burning sensations and discomfort associated with burning feet.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in frankincense capsules help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to conditions like burning feet, promoting overall skin health and well-being.

Pain-relieving properties: Frankincense contains boswellic acids, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes that can cause pain, making it a natural pain reliever for conditions like burning feet.
remedy_parts: Resin: The resin is a sticky, gummy substance secreted by the tree's trunk and branches, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help soothe burning pain in the feet.
Bark: The bark of the frankincense plant contains natural antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve discomfort caused by burning feet.
remedy_recipe: Grind dried boswellia serrata powder into a fine consistency using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of the ground boswellia serrata powder with 100 milligrams of glycerin and 10 milliliters of distilled water in a small bowl.
Stir the mixture well to combine ingredients and ensure uniformity, allowing it to sit for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Transfer the mixture to a capsule machine and fill empty capsules with the herbal mixture, yielding 60-70 capsules per batch.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature or refrigerate them for up to 2 years, ensuring consistent potency and freshness.

Barks: The barks contain glycosides and saponins that have analgesic and anti-pruritic effects, making them effective in soothing burning feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried ashwagandha root powder.
Grind 10 grams of haridra powder to a fine consistency using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of vasa powder with 10 grams of haridra powder in a bowl.
Fill empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the herbal mixture using a capsule machine.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container for up to six months.

Barks: The barks of the cloves plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and ease burning sensations in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried syzygium aromaticum bark from reputable sources for optimal potency.
Grind the dried bark into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix 5 grams of the powdered syzygium aromaticum bark with 95 grams of vegetable capsules in a clean glass bowl for 2 hours.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine or manually for 30 minutes.
Store the herbal syzygium aromaticum capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

Barks: Containing sesquiterpenes, these barks have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin on the feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality boswellia sacra resin from sustainably managed sources measuring 500 milligrams per capsule.
Dry the harvested resin in a warm dry place for at least 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Crush the dried resin into small pieces using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder ensuring consistent particle size.
Mix the crushed boswellia sacra with filler material such as rice flour or potato starch in a ratio of 1:5 achieving 500 milligrams total weight per capsule.
Fill clean capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to maintain potency for up to six months stored in an airtight container in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass capsules helps with burning feet because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The antiseptic and antimicrobial compounds in lemongrass also help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial or fungal infections that may contribute to burning sensations on the feet. Additionally, lemongrass has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing feelings of discomfort and pain associated with burning feet, providing long-lasting relief from this common condition.

Pain-relieving properties: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, helping to numb the pain associated with burning feet.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant-rich constituents in lemongrass capsules can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the appearance of discoloration and discomfort often associated with burning feet.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, being the underground stem, contains a higher concentration of essential oils and compounds that help to soothe and calm burning sensations in the feet.
Barks: Lemongrass barks are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can alleviate foot discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and dry them completely in the shade for 7-10 days.
Grind 30g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 100mg of vitamin e oil and 50ml of coconut oil to create a herbal blend.
Fill empty capsules with 500mg of the herbal blend and seal them immediately.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months before use.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Herbal sesame capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and pain in the feet.
Cooling and Soothing Properties: Sesame has natural cooling and soothing properties that can help to relieve itching, burning, and tingling sensations in the feet.

Rhyzomes: Containing antioxidant properties, sesame rhyzomes soothe and calm irritated skin on the feet.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried sesamum indicum seeds from a trusted supplier.
Grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of ground sesamum indicum powder with 500mg of organic silica gel in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the sesamum indicum-silica mixture, approximately 50mg per capsule.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Neem capsules helps with burning feet because of their natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules contain compounds that penetrate deep into the skin, soothing and calming irritated tissues. This relief from inflammation reduces discomfort and itching associated with burning feet. remedy_properties: Antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties: Neem's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation and prevent infection, which can contribute to the burning sensation in feet.
Antioxidant properties: Neem's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and tissues, reducing oxidative stress and pain associated with burning feet.

Analgesic and soothing properties: The compounds present in neem capsules have a natural analgesic effect, providing relief from burning sensations and discomfort, while also soothing the affected areas.

Buds: Neem buds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling caused by burning feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried neem bark and powder 100g into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered neem bark with 200g of gelatin in a stainless steel bowl for 5 minutes.
Add 20g of vitamin e oil and stir until well combined, then let it sit at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Use a capsule machine to fill pre-made capsules with the mixture, yielding 60-80 capsules, each containing 0.333g of neem bark powder and 0.25g of gelatin.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree capsules helps with burning feet because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The capsules contain oils from the Melaleuca alternifolia plant, which have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with burning feet. When taken orally, the tea tree oil is absorbed into the bloodstream and transported to the affected area, where it helps to soothe and calm irritated skin and nerves. This natural remedy can provide relief from the discomfort and itching that often accompanies burning feet.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat fungal and bacterial infections that may contribute to burning feet.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in tea tree oil, such as terpinen-4-ol, can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with burning feet.
Pain-relieving properties: Tea tree oil's analgesic and anesthetic properties can help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by burning feet, allowing for a more comfortable walking experience.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tea tree roots contain a high concentration of melaleuca oil, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help soothe and relieve burning sensations on the feet.
Buds: Tea tree buds have a higher concentration of cineole, a natural compound that helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing in skin irritations such as burning feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried leaves of melaleuca alternifolia and grind them into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine one part ground melaleuca alternifolia with two parts gelatin in a bowl and mix well for 5 minutes.
Add 300ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir until the gelatin is fully dissolved, taking approximately 10 minutes.
Fill empty capsule shells with the prepared herbal mixture and seal them tightly using a capsule filler machine or manual method.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 12 months to preserve potency.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to burning feet that are treated with herbal capsules are conditions such as numbness, tingling, and pain in hands or feet due to poor circulation. Herbal supplements like Ginkgo biloba, Turmeric, and Ashwagandha help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, relieving discomfort and numbness. Additionally, herbs like Capsicum, Ginger, and Boswellia may also be used to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with conditions like sciatica, arthritis, and fibromyalgia.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with burning feet is a blend of Turmeric, Ginger, and Aloe Vera. Turmeric contains curcumin, which reduces inflammation and alleviates pain, while Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and cool the skin. Aloe Vera, rich in vitamins and minerals, promotes skin health and hydration. This combination can help alleviate burning sensations and promote a sense of relief and comfort. Taking two capsules of each herbal supplement daily may provide optimal benefits.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Inflammopharmacology, herbal capsules can be beneficial for burning feet caused by diabetic neuropathy. The study employed a conventional management protocol for treating diabetic foot syndrome, which involves various therapies, and highlighted the effectiveness of plant-based treatments, including those derived from Globularia Arabica, Rhus coriaria L., Neolamarckia cadamba, and others, in promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation. By incorporating herbal capsules into treatment plans, individuals with diabetic foot syndrome may experience improved symptoms and reduced risk of complications, such as amputations, which is a significant concern, given that 25% of diabetic patients are at risk of lower limb amputations.
